title: Security Deposit
text: The Licensee/Tenant shall pay a refundable security deposit of INR {{security_deposit}} prior to or at the time of execution of this Agreement. The security deposit shall be held as security for the due performance of the Licensee/Tenant's obligations and shall not carry interest. The Licensor/Landlord shall refund the security deposit within thirty (30) days of the Licensee/Tenant vacating the Premises and delivering peaceful possession, after deducting any outstanding dues, unpaid license fee/rent, or costs of repairing damage caused by the Licensee/Tenant beyond fair wear and tear. The Licensor/Landlord shall furnish a written account of all deductions made.
name: Security Deposit

legal_basis.note: Forfeiture of deposit as penalty — must be genuine pre-estimate

jurisdiction_constraints.general: Model Tenancy Act 2021 S.11: security deposit capped at 2 months for residential, 6 months for commercial
jurisdiction_constraints.Maharashtra: Cap of 5% of property value for pagdi properties — consult local rent court rules
jurisdiction_constraints.Karnataka: Karnataka Rent Control Act — security deposit limits apply to regulated premises

dispute_hotspots: Security deposit refund delay — most common landlord-tenant dispute
Deductions from security deposit — landlord must prove damage; cannot deduct for normal wear and tear
Security deposit exceeding statutory limits under Model Tenancy Act 2021
Interest on security deposit — some states require landlord to pay interest
